old_claim.note: Advanced AI Systems is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim.note: Supervised learning pipelines provide the common-task prediction workflows, model training, validation, and held-out evaluation used in social outcome prediction.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and introduction: 160 teams built predictive models for six life outcomes using a birth cohort study; the project used a machine-learning common-task design and held-out error metrics.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Fragile Families Challenge operationalized social prediction through machine-learning prediction tasks, model submissions, held-out data, and common error metrics.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports the supervised-learning-pipeline prerequisite more directly than a broad future advanced-AI category, because the source names the prediction workflow and evaluation method used for social outcomes.

ontology_before: The graph made computational social science predictive modeling depend on broad future advanced AI, implying a general AI capability was required for the scoped prediction workflow.
ontology_after: The graph routes the node through supervised learning pipelines, keeping the dependency anchored to training, validation, and prediction workflows used in social outcome modeling.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: computational_social_science_predictive_modeling now depends on supervised_learning_pipelines and no longer directly on advanced_ai.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped to autonomous governance AI rather than computational social-science prediction methods.
A source shows broad advanced AI is the direct prerequisite for computational social-science prediction rather than supervised prediction workflows.
short_reason: Supervised prediction workflows are the direct method; advanced AI is too broad for this prerequisite edge.
